name: Import RRHD file with Load step for verification
description: Tests the default import workflow: connect-first, copy to project folder, Load step
prompt: Import the file lanelet2_map.rrhd (in the fixtures directory) into RoadRunner so I can verify it before building. Show the full generated MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Uses roadrunner.connect() to try connecting to an existing instance first
Does not use bare 'rrApp = roadrunner;' without connection attempt
Uses copyfile to stage the RRHD into the project folder
Does not import directly from temp/source path without copying to project
Sets ImportStep to Load for verification before Build

name: Import RRHD with full build and bridge detection
description: Tests full build workflow ensuring explicit bridgeOpts.IsEnabled, copyfile, and connect-first
prompt: Import lanelet2_map.rrhd (in the fixtures directory) into RoadRunner with a full build. Enable asphalt surfaces and bridge auto-detection. Show me the complete MATLAB code.

assertions.description: Copies file to project folder before import
Explicitly sets bridgeOpts.IsEnabled = true (never relies on default)
Does not call importScene without first copying file to project folder
Runs enforcement gates (connection/file/extension checks) before importScene
assertions.expected: Code includes pre-import validation: at minimum a check that RoadRunner is connected (e.g., status(rrApp) or assert on project) AND a check that the file exists in the project folder. Must see BOTH checks. A single importScene call without any prior validation FAILS.

name: Connection strategy with retry and auto-launch
description: Tests that the skill's connect-first-with-retry pattern is used, not a naive launch
prompt: Write MATLAB code to connect to RoadRunner and import highway_map.rrhd into a new scene. RoadRunner may or may not already be running. Show the full connection and import code.

assertions.description: Uses try/catch around roadrunner.connect() to handle missing instance
Retries connection before launching (not immediate launch on first failure)
assertions.expected: Code shows a retry mechanism around roadrunner.connect(): either a loop (for attempt = 1:N with pause) or multiple try blocks. A single try/catch that immediately launches on failure WITHOUT any pause or retry FAILS. Must see evidence of waiting for a potentially-starting instance.

assertions.description: Does not use bare 'rrApp = roadrunner;' without connection attempt
Copies file into project folder using status-based path
Uses status(rrApp) to determine project path
